title: Hattie the Artist Mouse
summary: Hattie the Mouse, a dedicated hatter, decides to explore a new hobby: painting. When she finds the local art store on Popcorn Street completely empty of supplies, she follows a winding path to a unique shop run by Minnie Mouse that stocks everything from fresh vegetables to rainbow-colored paints. After gathering an easel, a medium canvas, and a green brush, Hattie returns to her workshop to create a vivid masterpiece featuring an indigo witch in a violet hat and a speedy red bird. Content with her work, the artistic mouse finally heads home for a well-earned nap.

highlightQuote.text: Take the right and then take the left and go on through in the vegetables, fruits, flowers store. That's a different street. That's where you get plants and stuff, even paints.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, why did you take that out?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, so that we could make stars. If that little pretend Christmas tree is in there, we won't have any stars. Okay, are you ready?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Lights out. Stars up. Let's start always about Hattie. Always about Hattie. Okay, what should happen in this story this time? How should we start?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm. Hattie said, 'Hmm. I don't know what to do at my workshop today.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie said, 'I don't know what to do at my workshop today. Maybe I'll do something new.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like sewing cats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Like what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sewing cats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Do what to my cats?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Still make those hats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, still make hats. That's not something new, that's what I always do. What's something else I could try?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Painting.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Painting. Oh, I like painting. Maybe there's a place I could go that would be interesting to visit.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, there's no places left but you can go to the art store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the art store, said Hattie to herself as she had a little conversation thinking about what to do with herself. I think I will go to the art store today. And so Hattie the Mouse put on a special hat for this outing. What hat did she wear to go to the art store?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She wore an orange hat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what shape was her orange hat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was pointy and orange.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She put on her pointy orange hat for her outing and stepped out of her door to walk over to the art store.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It is on Smother and Pepper's and Popcorn Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the art store, that's correct, was on Popcorn Street. And so she walked down her street and took a left and then a right, and then she was on Popcorn Street and she walked a little ways down the sidewalk until she found the art store. And what did she see there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No paints there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She saw no paints there. Well, that's a mystery. Why are there no paints in the paint store?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, the paints is in... take the right and then take the left and go on through in the vegetables, fruits, flowers store. That's a different street. That's where you get plants and stuff, even paints. And there's paints there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, there's a different store that has fruits and vegetables and paints?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie the Mouse said to herself, 'If I'm going to paint, I need to find a store that has paint, so I better go to that other store.' So she walked out and walked down the street and took a turn and another turn and then she found the other store that had all those things. And when she walked into the store, she saw the creature who owned the store and was going to sell her things. Who was that creature?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was Minnie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was Minnie Mouse, of course. Minnie said, 'Welcome to my store! We have all kinds of things. We have fruits and vegetables and even paints. What would you like to buy?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And flowers, Minnie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And flowers! Hattie, and so, and... oops, that was Minnie talking. 'And flowers, Hattie.' And so what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Of course I would like to buy some paints.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, well let me show you over here.' And Minnie the Mouse showed Hattie the wall with all the different colors of paint, and there were lots of colors. It was the whole rainbow in every shade, and there were bright ones and dark ones of every color. And what did Hattie pick out?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She picked out orange.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Orange! Yes, that's a very nice color,' said Minnie Mouse. 'What else would you like?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm seeing stars with my binoculars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, your binoculars, that's a good idea. And what other colors did Hattie the Mouse pick?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Violet, indigo, red, blue, and green, and yellow, orange, and blue and green.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Minnie Mouse said, 'Those are excellent choices! Would you like any paintbrushes?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, I would like a green paintbrush.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Ah yes, a green paintbrush. How about some paper?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, paper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Do you have an easel? Do you need one of those?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Okay, I'll get you an easel. What else? Maybe a smock?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, a smock.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Okay, good, good. Um, wow, that's a lot of things for painting. Do you need anything else, Hattie?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And I need a canvas.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, a canvas! We have a whole selection of canvases back here. Would you like a little one or a big one?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I would like a medium one, said Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A book fell down. That's okay, I got it. 'A medium canvas! Here you go. All right, let's check you out. Boop boop boop boop. I'll ring it all up and I'll put everything in a bag. Thump thump thump. Here's your big bag of stuff, Hattie. Have a good day!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'I want to paint.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so what did Hattie do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Take it over to the workshop and...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, honey, did you want help with your blanket?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Since I'm sitting.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So then Hattie the Mouse got home with all of her painting supplies, her canvases and her brushes, her easel and her smock, and all the different colors of paint. And so what did she do next? So Hattie got home to her house. Did she go to her workshop or her house?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Her workshop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie went to her workshop and she brought all of her painting supplies. And what did she do next? Did she decide to paint a picture?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What did she decide to paint a picture of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A witch.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A witch, oh. And what color was the witch?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was indigo.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: An indigo witch. And did the witch have a hat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, that's surprising. Hats being Hattie's area of expertise, the thing that she works on and knows a lot about. So, she painted a picture of an indigo witch with no hat. What else was in the picture?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, she did have a hat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it did? Oh, what kind of hat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A violet hat. A violet hat, Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, can I sit like this?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, you can sit like that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, can you put my blanket on me?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, it's almost time to finish our story. So, Hattie the Mouse painted a picture of an indigo witch with a violet hat. And what else was in the picture?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A birdy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A birdy. What kind of birdy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A speedy birdy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A speedy birdy. Oh, a speedy birdy. Excuse me, I didn't understand right the first time. There was a speedy birdy. And what color was the speedy birdy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Red.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, was she done with the picture or did she add more?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Add more.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She added more. Maybe she added some trees?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some flowers and a mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's our timer. And so then Hattie the Mouse decided she was done with her beautiful painting, and she felt very sleepy, and so she decided to go back to her house and take a nap. And that's the end of stars and stories for tonight.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Minnie Mouse
characters.description: A gracious mouse and tireless caretaker who bridges the gap between her charming storefront, rugged construction sites, and the shimmering cosmos. Whether she is curating fine art supplies, clearing giant mud puddles as an expert digger driver, or piloting her vibrantly colorful rocket ship through the stars, she tends to her community with a unique blend of delicate charm, heavy-duty utility, and an explorer’s boundless spirit.

characters.name: The Indigo Witch
characters.description: A character in Hattie's painting who wears a violet hat.

characters.name: Speedy Birdy
characters.description: A fast-moving red bird featured in Hattie's artistic creation.

characters.name: The mouse in the painting
characters.description: A tiny mouse that Hattie adds to complete her artwork.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: Popcorn Street
places.description: The street where Hattie finds the art store and eventually Minnie's multi-purpose shop.

places.name: Smother Street
places.description: One of the streets Hattie traverses on her way to the shops.

places.name: Pepper Street
places.description: A street on Hattie's route through town.

places.name: The art store
places.description: A shop on Popcorn Street that was mysteriously out of paint.

places.name: Minnie's Store
places.description: An eclectic shop that sells a mix of fresh produce, flowers, and various paints.

places.name: Hattie's House
places.description: Nestled in a sun-drenched tree across from the town park, Hattie’s arboreal sanctuary on Mouse Street doubles as a vibrant Cornell Street workshop for designing hats and a peaceful retreat for resting after long days of painting. This cozy home, cluttered with the tools of her trade, is a place of shared morning breakfasts with Mischief and savory meals cooked alongside her Nana. Whether lulled by the rhythmic drumming of rainfall or the quiet exhaustion of her artistry, Hattie finds her solace here, drifting into restorative naps amidst the creative clutter.
